summary.intent: Add bundled default skills to Moltis by copying vetted skills from Hermes Agent and OpenClaw, embedding them in the binary like web UI assets, with category support and a web UI component to browse them by category.
summary.outcome: 101 skills (69 from Hermes, 32 from OpenClaw) are now embedded in the Moltis binary via include_dir!, discoverable via CompositeSkillDiscoverer with user skills overriding bundled ones, cleaned of Hermes-specific references, annotated with origin metadata and requires: frontmatter, and browsable by category in the web UI.

repo: Moltis already had a SkillSource enum and SkillDiscoverer trait in crates/skills/src/ — the bundled system extended existing patterns rather than introducing new ones.
The web asset embedding pattern (include_dir! with dev-mode filesystem fallback) is the canonical way to embed static content in Moltis binaries.
SkillSource had exhaustive matches in discover.rs and watcher.rs that needed updating when adding the Bundled variant.
The bundled-skills feature must be forwarded through the dependency chain: skills → tools → gateway → cli Cargo.toml.
Hermes skills use metadata:/hermes:/dependencies:/prerequisites: frontmatter that is not Moltis-native and needed to be stripped; Moltis uses name/description/origin/requires/platforms.
Sidecar files (references/, templates/, scripts/, assets/) alongside SKILL.md are supported by include_dir! and contribute the bulk of line count (~144k of 162k total).
mlops skills have 3-level deep directory nesting (mlops/training/axolotl/) requiring recursive discovery logic rather than the initial 2-level assumption.
The services.rs SkillListEntry needed a category field added to expose category grouping to the web UI via the REST API.

code.finding: BundledSkillStore uses include_dir! in release mode and direct filesystem reads in dev mode for instant iteration, same three-tier pattern as web assets.
CompositeSkillDiscoverer merges FS + bundled sources with name-based deduplication; user skills (Personal/Project) take priority over bundled by appearing earlier in the chain.
Had an exhaustive match on SkillSource that needed a Bundled arm (no-op, bundled skills are not watched for filesystem changes).
ReadSkillTool gained a with_bundled() constructor and read_bundled() function that serves content from memory for bundled skills instead of filesystem.
Added SkillSource::Bundled variant, SkillOrigin struct (source/url/commit/copied_at), origin field on SkillMetadata, and category: Option<String> field for directory-derived categorization.
EnabledSkillsTable extended to group skills by category with filter pills; category field added to the Skill TypeScript type.
Category pill and badge CSS classes added alongside existing skills component styles.

workflow: Python scripts are more reliable than sed for injecting structured YAML frontmatter into markdown files; sed truncated files when the pattern spanned lines.
cargo check is sufficient to validate compilation across feature chains without incurring full test build costs (which hit disk space limits in worktrees).
Auditing bundled scripts for security: scripts/ sidecars are passive text served to the LLM, not auto-executed — actual execution requires the agent to explicitly write+run them via exec tool.
Background bash commands with output redirection hung in this environment; running commands directly is more reliable.
Node modules are not pre-installed in git worktrees; npm install required before TypeScript type-checking or Vite builds.

friction: Initial sed-based origin metadata injection corrupted SKILL.md files by truncating them; required re-copying from Hermes and rewriting with Python.
Disk space exhaustion in the worktree prevented full cargo test runs for gateway and CLI crates — only skills and tools crates could be tested.
Background bash commands with output file redirection hung silently, requiring multiple retry approaches to compare skill lists between repos.
Arc import was already in scope in services.rs but the added code used fully-qualified std::sync::Arc::new causing a clippy lint failure.
The bundled skills directory was initially designed for 2-level nesting (category/skill/) but mlops has 3-level nesting (mlops/subcategory/skill/) requiring a recursive discovery rewrite.
Node modules absent from worktree required an npm install step before TypeScript validation could proceed.
open_items: Remaining 5 'Nous Hermes' model name references in godmode/SKILL.md are correct but the godmode skill itself (red-teaming jailbreak) warrants a policy review for inclusion in default bundled skills.

~25k lines of unsloth reference files (llms-full.md, llms-txt.md) and ~15k lines of powerpoint XSD schemas dominate the binary size contribution; these could be trimmed if binary size becomes a concern.
OpenClaw extensions/ skills (Feishu, QQBot, ACP) and platform-specific skills were skipped but could be added as opt-in skills for users on those platforms.
The GitHub skills still contain some ~/.hermes/ path references in example code that were not caught by the bulk cleanup (mentioned in session but deferred).
A mechanism to disable individual bundled skills from the web UI (rather than only overriding by name) was not implemented.
Category metadata is derived purely from directory structure; skills copied from openclaw into existing categories may not have the right category if their source category differed.
